metadata.seed: I keep overthinking everything at work, and I'm scared I'm going to get fired even though nobody said anything.

patient.query: I keep overthinking everything at work, and I'm scared I'm going to get fired even though nobody said anything.

automatic_thoughts: I'm scared I'm going to get fired
I keep overthinking everything

entailment: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're experiencing a lot of worry about your job, and it's taking up a lot of mental space. What makes you believe that getting fired is a likely outcome, even without any direct feedback?

patient.query: Yeah, it does feel like I'm constantly on edge about my job. I think it’s just this nagging feeling that I'm not good enough, you know? Like, I see others succeeding, and I’m just waiting for someone to realize I’m not measuring up. It’s exhausting.

automatic_thoughts: I'm not good enough
waiting for someone to realize I'm not measuring up

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're carrying a heavy burden of self-doubt when it comes to your work. What makes you feel like you're not measuring up compared to your colleagues? Let’s explore that a bit more.

patient.query: I guess it’s just little things, like when I hear my coworkers talking about their achievements or getting praise from our boss. I can't help but feel like I'm falling behind. And then I start overthinking every little mistake I make, like it’s proof that I don’t belong there. It makes me anxious just thinking about it.

automatic_thoughts: I'm falling behind
every little mistake is proof that I don’t belong there

neutral: Fear of being in a small group (finding)
Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're experiencing a lot of distress when you compare yourself to your coworkers. What makes you believe that these moments of feeling behind are actually true, and what other explanations might there be for why you feel this way?

patient.query: I don’t know, it just feels so real in the moment. I mean, I’ve always had this voice in my head telling me I'm not as capable as others. It’s like I can’t shake that feeling, even when I do get positive feedback. Maybe it’s just me being too hard on myself? But then I worry it’s just an excuse I’m making to avoid facing the

automatic_thoughts: I'm not as capable as others
Maybe it’s just me being too hard on myself
It's just an excuse I’m making to avoid facing the

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like this voice has been with you for a while, and it can be really challenging to dismiss, even when things go well. What makes you believe that voice is true, and what would it take for you to question its accuracy?

patient.query: Yeah, it really feels like it’s stuck with me forever. I guess it’s just that nagging thought that comes up every time I face a challenge. It’s like I’ve always been told I need to prove myself. Questioning it feels risky, like I might uncover something that’s even worse about myself. It’s easier to just accept it, I think.

automatic_thoughts: I need to prove myself
questioning it feels risky
I might uncover something worse about myself
it's easier to just accept it

contradiction: Turning against the self - mental defense mechanism (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like this expectation to prove yourself has been a consistent pressure, and it’s understandable that questioning it feels unsettling. What makes you think that exploring this belief might uncover something worse about yourself? Perhaps we can examine where this idea of needing to prove yourself came from.

patient.query: I guess it’s just this fear that if I dig deeper, I’ll find out I really am as incompetent as I sometimes feel. Like, if I look too closely, I might have to face the reality that I’m just not cut out for this. It’s been a part of me for so long; it almost feels safer to just keep it. But I’m also tired of living like

automatic_thoughts: if I dig deeper, I’ll find out I really am as incompetent as I sometimes feel
if I look too closely, I might have to face the reality that I’m just not cut out for this

neutral: Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're worried that looking closely at things might confirm a feeling you already have about yourself. What makes you think that digging deeper would necessarily reveal something negative, and what would that look like?

patient.query: I guess it’s just this long-standing belief that I’ve carried for years, like if I really examine my thoughts or feelings, they’ll just prove how inadequate I am. What that would look like? Maybe it would mean realizing I’ve been fooling myself this whole time, thinking I was better than I really am. It’s scary to imagine confronting that and having to live with that truth.

automatic_thoughts: if I really examine my thoughts or feelings, they’ll just prove how inadequate I am
realizing I’ve been fooling myself this whole time
thinking I was better than I really am

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're worried that looking closely at your thoughts and feelings might uncover something difficult about yourself. What makes you believe that examining your experiences would inevitably lead to this negative conclusion?

patient.query: I don’t know, it just feels like a gut instinct, you know? Every time I’ve tried in the past to really look at what’s going on, it’s been tough. It’s almost like I’m afraid of what I’ll find. There’s this little voice that keeps saying, “What if you really are as flawed as you fear?” It’s like there’s comfort in just

automatic_thoughts: What if you really are as flawed as you fear?

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're caught between a desire to understand and a worry about what that understanding might bring up. What makes it so difficult to look at what's going on, and what do you imagine might be so uncomfortable about what you might discover?

patient.query: Yeah, that’s definitely part of it. It’s like if I dig too deep, I might uncover feelings that I’ve been trying to keep buried. I guess I’m scared that I’ll find out I’m not just dealing with my job issues, but something bigger, something really wrong with me. I’m just not sure I’m ready to handle that. It feels safer to just keep focusing

automatic_thoughts: I might uncover feelings that I’ve been trying to keep buried
I’m scared that I’ll find out I’m not just dealing with my job issues
something bigger, something really wrong with me
I’m just not sure I’m ready to handle that

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're concerned that exploring these feelings might lead to something overwhelming, and that you're questioning your ability to manage it. What makes you believe you wouldn't be able to handle what you might find?

patient.query: I think it’s just this history of feeling lost when I confront my emotions. Like, every time I think I’m making progress, I end up hitting a wall, and it feels like I’m right back at square one. I guess I worry that if I uncover more stuff, I might just drown in it. I don’t want to go through that struggle again. It’s exhausting.

automatic_thoughts: I’m right back at square one
I might just drown in it
I don’t want to go through that struggle again

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're concerned that exploring your emotions will just lead to more setbacks and a feeling of being stuck. What makes you feel that confronting your emotions always leads to hitting a wall?
